frozen_components: LLM (used as-is for multiple inference roles)
architecture.summary: KG-EGV is a QA framework that integrates knowledge graphs with LLMs through a four-step pipeline: sentence segmentation, graph retrieval, EGV (an LLM inference framework), and backward updating. The EGV component orchestrates LLM inference across six stages (generation expansion, expansion evaluation, document re-ranking, re-ranking evaluation, answer generation, answer verification), where the LLM assumes multiple roles (generator, re-ranker, evaluator, verifier) to synthesize diverse evidence from both retrieval and generation, combining retrieved KG components with LLM-generated reasoning to produce final answers with verification.

biblio.title: KG-EGV: A Framework for Question Answering with Integrated Knowledge Graphs and Large Language Models

claims.statement: KG-EGV achieves state-of-the-art performance in answer accuracy and evidence quality on widely used benchmarks
EGV enables comprehensive reasoning beyond retrieval by synthesizing diverse evidence, which is often unattainable via retrieval alone due to noise or hallucinations
By combining the strengths of retrieval-based and generation-based evidence, KG-EGV achieves greater flexibility and accuracy in evidence gathering and answer formulation
